microglial_activation: Elevated microglial pro-inflammatory markers (TNF-α, IL-6, IL-1β within CNS)
Elevated pro-inflammatory metabolites (tryptophan → kynurenine via iron-dependent IDO)

ecologicalFeatures.hypoxia: Dysbiotic shift toward facultative anaerobes (E. coli, Bacteroides fragilis) suggests local oxygen depletion. Loss of oxygen-sensitive Faecalibacterium prausnitzii indicates anaerobic dominance.
ecologicalFeatures.biofilm: Pseudomonadota and Bacteroides fragilis form polysaccharide biofilms that sequester metals (iron via siderophore bioaccumulation, zinc via PSA accumulation) and shield internal pathogens from immune attack and antimicrobials.
ecologicalFeatures.estrogen_recirculation: Dysbiotic expansion of beta-glucuronidase producers (E. coli, Bacteroides fragilis, Candida) amplifies estrogen recirculation loop. Result: elevated circulating estrogens that suppress IL-17-dependent immunity (estrogen inhibits IL-17 differentiation) and amplify Th2 dominance. Secondary effect: estrogen-dependent fungal growth acceleration in Candida comorbidity.
ecologicalFeatures.functional_shielding: Bacterial biofilms + fungal hyphae (Candida) create anaerobic microenvironments that protect internal pathogens. Dysbiotic taxa sequester metals within biofilm matrix, denying them to beneficial competitors and to host absorption. Result: dual phenotype of systemic metal dysregulation (host perspective) and local metal hoarding (dysbiotic niche perspective).
ecologicalFeatures.pH_shift: Loss of butyrate production → reduced colonic pH acidification → shift away from acidophilic Bacteroidetes toward neutral-pH-tolerant Firmicutes and Pseudomonadota.
ecologicalFeatures.mucus_degradation: Loss of mucus-fermenting Akkermansia muciniphila and mucin-degrading Bacteroidetes → mucus layer thinning → increased bacterial translocation across intestinal epithelium → endotoxemia (LPS translocation).

iron_dependent.source: Dysbiotic taxa (E. coli, via TLR4 stimulation of host macrophages); also kynurenine-pathway bacteria
iron_dependent.function: Tryptophan → kynurenine; depletes tryptophan precursor for serotonin and AhR signaling; produces neurotoxic kynurenine; drives Th17 differentiation
iron_dependent.consequence: Reduced serotonin availability, reduced AhR agonism, elevated neuroinflammation

iron_dependent.source: E. coli, Pseudomonas, Klebsiella, other Gram-negatives
iron_dependent.function: Scavenge host iron; sequester iron within biofilm; deprive competitors and host of bioavailable iron
iron_dependent.consequence: Systemic iron dysregulation; hepcidin elevation; impaired SCFA/metabolite production by iron-dependent enzymes

iron_dependent.function: Lyse red blood cells; release heme iron for scavenging
iron_dependent.consequence: Red blood cell destruction, iron release, ROS production via heme Fenton chemistry

iron_dependent.source: Dysbiotic taxa (E. coli and other pathogens); also host mitochondria
iron_dependent.function: Pyruvate → acetyl-CoA; central to SCFA production and energy metabolism
iron_dependent.consequence: Dysbiotic taxa: upregulation in iron-rich environment enables growth. Beneficial taxa (Faecalibacterium): downregulation due to iron sequestration → loss of butyrate production.
Pathogens resist oxidative stress; dysbiotic taxa can persist despite host ROS defense
Enables pathogenic persistence under oxidative stress

iron_dependent.source: Host mitochondria (not microbiota, but critical to mechanism)
iron_dependent.consequence: Dysbiosis-driven mitochondrial dysfunction (via endotoxemia, nutrient depletion) → reduced ATP, reduced synaptic plasticity, reduced neuronal resilience

zinc_dependent.source: Some Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains (GABA producers)
zinc_dependent.consequence: Dysbiotic microbiota lose zinc-dependent GABA production → reduced GABA availability, reduced anxiolytic signaling
Pathogenic bacteria can modify LPS to evade immune recognition

zinc_dependent.source: Central to SCFA production (butyrate, propionate, acetate synthesis)
zinc_dependent.consequence: Dysbiotic shift away from zinc-dependent SCFA producers → reduced butyrate, impaired epigenetic regulation, impaired barrier support
Dysbiotic proteolysis → altered amino acid availability; tryptophan loss; altered neurotransmitter precursor pools

zinc_dependent.function: Tissue invasion; barrier disruption; immune evasion
zinc_dependent.consequence: Fungal pathogenesis; barrier breakdown; dysbiotic persistence

nickel_dependent.source: H. pylori, some Helicobacter species, some Klebsiella, Proteus
nickel_dependent.function: Urea → ammonia + CO₂; pH neutralization; pathogenic persistence in acidic gastric environment
nickel_dependent.consequence: Enables H. pylori persistence; dysbiotic overgrowth in nickel-rich environment

nickel_dependent.source: Multiple anaerobic bacteria; dysbiotic expansion of H₂-producing taxa
nickel_dependent.function: H₂ production from formate; energy metabolism under anaerobic conditions
nickel_dependent.consequence: Dysbiotic shift toward H₂-producing, nickel-dependent anaerobes

nickel_dependent.enzyme: Methyl-coenzyme M reductase (if methanogenic archaea present)
nickel_dependent.source: Methanogenic archaea (rare but possible in severe dysbiosis)
nickel_dependent.consequence: Dysbiotic persistence; methane production (marker of fermentation-dominant dysbiosis)

cadmium_dependent (adverse).consequence: If cadmium is elevated (environmental exposure), dysbiotic taxa tolerant to cadmium toxicity have competitive advantage → cadmium-enriched dysbiosis selection
Dysbiotic expansion of stress-adapted, metal-hoarding taxa

enriched.role: Adherent-invasive E. coli (AIEC) — mucosa-invasive, siderophore-dependent, iron-scavenging
Pro-inflammatory, oxygen-consuming, enriched across multiple cohorts
Metal-resistant, consistently pro-inflammatory
Mucin-degrading, barrier-damaging
Mixed — some strains pathogenic (BFT toxin), others commensal
Fungal component — biofilm formation, functional shielding, interkingdom interactions
Pathobiont enriched by heavy metal exposure (As, Pb, Hg)

depleted.role: Primary butyrate producer — most consistently depleted taxon across all CD cohorts; essential for arsenic detoxification; low abundance predicts post-surgical recurrence
Butyrate and propionate producer — consistently reduced
SCFA producers — lost in iron-rich pro-inflammatory environment; Mendelian randomization confirms protective causal role
Mucus-layer specialist — depleted; Mendelian randomization shows causal protective association
SCFA producer — Mendelian randomization confirms protective causal role

enriched.role: Primary pathogen — all Zn/Fe/Ni enzymes, estrogen deconjugation via beta-glucuronidase
Zn-dependent BFT toxin, iron piracy via siderophores, beta-glucuronidase, strict anaerobe indicating hypoxia
Group B Strep — Zn/Ni critical cofactors, beta-glucuronidase positive, iron piracy
Zn/Ni-dependent enzymes, oxygen consumption creating local hypoxia, NOT involved in estrogen deconjugation
Functional shielding — biofilms consume O₂, co-aggregates with F. nucleatum and E. coli, enhances epithelial colonization of Strep, NOT noise
Enriched in gut/peritoneum but DEPLETED in vaginal cavity — translocation likely; nickel-dependent, glyoxalase-positive

depleted.role: SCFA producers — lost competitive advantage in iron-rich pro-inflammatory environment
SCFA producers — lacked defense systems to survive iron-rich environment

enriched.role: Elevated B. fragilis — beta-glucuronidase, LPS production, Th17 skewing
Gram-negative LPS producers — drive intestinal permeability and systemic inflammation
Molecular mimicry with thyroid peroxidase (TPO); immune dysregulation
Mucin-degrading; LPS production; associated with intestinal permeability
Opportunistic expansion — Zn/Fe/Ni-dependent virulence enzymes, LPS translocation

depleted.role: Primary butyrate producer — loss impairs Treg induction and barrier integrity
SCFA producers — loss drives HDAC inhibition failure and NF-kB activation
Mucus-layer specialist — depletion drives barrier dysfunction and Treg loss
Paradoxical — generally protective but dysbiotic expansion pattern reported in some cohorts
